items.label: Tommy Fleetwood (+1450):
items.body: One of the betting market leaders this week, with a polished all-around game and the kind of approach play that tends to separate contenders at TPC San Antonio’s demanding Oaks Course.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+1600):
items.body: A top-tier ball striker who can overwhelm long par-4s and convert enough birdies on the easier scoring holes to contend if his putter cooperates.

items.label: Russell Henley (+1600):
items.body: Reliable tee-to-green performer and one of the more consistent players in the field, making him a strong fit for a precision-heavy test with difficult greens and tight scoring margins.

items.label: Robert MacIntyre (+1650):
items.body: Left-handed power and steady iron play give him real upside at a course that rewards both distance control and patience on a typically stern setup.

items.label: Si Woo Kim (+1750):
items.body: Strong fit on positional courses because of his accuracy and creativity, with enough short-game skill to stay in the mix through a demanding weekend.

items.label: Collin Morikawa (+1800):
items.body: Elite iron player whose ceiling rises on courses where approach shots and wedge control matter more than raw scoring volume.

items.label: Jordan Spieth (+1900):
items.body: Texas native with major-winning pedigree and a strong record in this event, bringing plenty of upside if the putter gets hot on home soil.

items.label: Hideki Matsuyama (+2300):
items.body: High-end ball striker capable of separating from the field with sustained tee-to-green excellence and enough scoring punch to win on a tough par-72.

items.label: Maverick McNealy (+2400):
items.body: A balanced all-around player whose steady form and well-rounded profile make him a live mid-range option at a venue that punishes mistakes.

items.label: Final Masters Spot:
items.body: This is the final event before Augusta for players still trying to earn the last Masters invitation, which raises the pressure and the stakes across the board.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Brian Harman returns as the defending champion after winning last year’s Valero Texas Open and looks to repeat at TPC San Antonio.

items.label: Deep Top End:
items.body: The field includes several top-20 players such as Tommy Fleetwood, Ludvig Åberg, Russell Henley, Hideki Matsuyama, Jordan Spieth, and J.J. Spaun, giving the event an unusually strong feel for a week before a major.

items.label: Texas Swing Finish:
items.body: The tournament closes the PGA Tour’s Texas stretch before the season turns to the Masters, so many players are using it as a final tune-up on a long, demanding course.

items.label: Recent Winners:
items.body: Recent champions at TPC San Antonio include Brian Harman in 2025, Akshay Bhatia in 2024, Corey Conners in 2023, J.J. Spaun in 2022, and Jordan Spieth in 2021.

items.label: Masters Path:
items.body: The Valero Texas Open has long carried added significance because it can provide the last remaining path into the Masters field for a player who is not otherwise exempt.

items.label: Course Demands:
items.body: TPC San Antonio usually rewards a complete game rather than a single skill, with strong ball striking and disciplined decision-making typically separating the winner from the rest.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: TPC San Antonio (Oaks Course), San Antonio, Texas; par 72, 7,438 yards, and one of the longer PGA Tour venues.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: Pete Dye and Greg Norman designed the Oaks Course to test patience and precision, with four par-5s, four par-3s, and 10 par-4s, plus a demanding closing stretch.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $9.8 million total purse, with $1,764,000 to the winner.

items.label: Format and Dates:
items.body: 72-hole stroke play with a 36-hole cut to the low 65 and ties, played Thursday, April 2 through Sunday, April 5, 2026.

items.label: TV:
items.body: Golf Channel and NBC are expected to share U.S. television coverage, with PGA Tour Live on ESPN+ providing streaming coverage.

items.label: Thursday and Friday:
items.body: Early-round streaming covers the opening tee times, with Golf Channel carrying the main afternoon TV windows.

items.label: Saturday and Sunday:
items.body: Weekend coverage shifts toward NBC for the main broadcast windows, while PGA Tour Live continues to carry featured groups and full streaming coverage.
